Description: Infrastructure for AI-assisted clinical research enabling natural-language exploration of ICU datasets (MIMIC-IV, eICU). Operates as an MCP server (Claude Desktop, Cursor) and Python API with clinician-authored Agent Skills modules. Evaluation framework: L1 SQL accuracy, L2 clinical artifact generation, L3 research reproduction. Preprint target April 2026.

Project Name: Lab-MAE: Foundation Model for Lab Data
Description: Transformer-based foundation model creating vector embeddings of laboratory test data from MIMIC. Contextualizes lab values (e.g., creatinine 3.6 means something different per patient/time). Provides a knowledge graph of numeric clinical data capturing physiological states.

Project Name: LabTube: 3D Temporal Modeling of ICU Labs
Description: VideoMAE-style modeling of ICU lab time series. Elevates time to a structural dimension ('the tube'). Unifies time and lab space in one 3D tensor for spatio-temporal modeling. Described as successor to Lab-MAE.
Notes / Duplicates: Related to Lab-MAE (explicit successor). Kept as a separate project.

Project Name: SLICES: SSL Objectives for ICU Representation
Description: Modular framework comparing self-supervised learning (SSL) objectives on ICU patient representation tasks. Master's thesis project.
Improving snapshot embeddings of EHR data using multimodal data. Version 1 in final stages.
Leveraging the vector embedding approach for integration of multimodal data in cardiovascular disease.
Pipeline to extract vector embeddings from pre-trained transformers. Final stages of v1.
Notes / Duplicates: Related to the broader Vector Embedding team workstream. Kept as a separate project.
Team Members: Ahram Han (https://www.notion.so/Ahram-Han-318e4faff87f8121af18dd304596944a?pvs=21), Takeshi Tohyama (https://www.notion.so/Takeshi-Tohyama-318e4faff87f81e2a9adf4ac0de20d03?pvs=21), Norman Pedersen (https://www.notion.so/Norman-Pedersen-318e4faff87f81be9a6edf95f2c888c0?pvs=21)

Project Name: Bias in Omics Data Beyond Non-Representativeness
Description: Systematic review investigating sources of bias across the full lifecycle of OMICS data (genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics, metabolomics). Covers data production, technical/instrumental, computational/analytical, and reporting stages. ~179 articles reviewed.
Key Team Members: Kezia, Marlene, Marianna, Amir Hossein, Mohammad, Jehad, Sebastian, Osama, Yichun

Description: Measuring how pain is documented across clinical notes (BRIDGE) and standardized exam questions (WorldMedQA) to uncover systematic bias in data and models.

Description: Comparing how concepts like 'pain' are represented across cultures and languages using BRIDGE and WorldMedQA datasets (~30 countries).

Notes / Duplicates: Related to MedScope (Boya). Same datasets, same bias theme. Kept as a separate project.

Project Name: Impact Analysis of Prediction Models (Causal Inference)
Description: Evaluating whether prediction models improve patient outcomes before clinical implementation. Methods: target trial emulation, interrupted time series, difference-in-difference, and other causal inference approaches.
Team Members: Takeshi Tohyama (https://www.notion.so/Takeshi-Tohyama-318e4faff87f81e2a9adf4ac0de20d03?pvs=21), Ahram Han (https://www.notion.so/Ahram-Han-318e4faff87f8121af18dd304596944a?pvs=21), Norman Pedersen (https://www.notion.so/Norman-Pedersen-318e4faff87f81be9a6edf95f2c888c0?pvs=21)

Description: Measuring model drift over time using MIMIC data.
